hero.description: Turkey's new regime exempts foreign income and capital gains of new tax residents for up to twenty years. We analyse what this means for your portfolio, business sale, holding company and crypto assets — and where Austrian exit tax rules apply.

advantage.title: People buy from their situation — not because of a law.
advantage.intro: Turkey is the instrument. The real question is always: what happens to my specific wealth?
bullets: Entrepreneurs & Holdings — GmbH shares, distributions, participation structures
Founders before exit — SaaS, agency, e-commerce: structure residency before the sale, not after
Portfolio & ETF — 500k to several million in stocks and funds, what stays, what becomes free
Crypto investors — BTC, ETH, SOL. High unrealised gains — checked, not promised
Family Offices — securities, funds, participations across multiple levels
Wealthy Austrians — own vertical, significantly less competitive in the relocation market

whyNow.title: Why This Matters Now
whyNow.p1: Turkey's new tax regime is based on a legislative initiative announced in April 2026. As of June 2026: in parliamentary process. The final legislation and administrative practice will be decisive.
whyNow.p2: Those who have not been tax-resident in Turkey for the past three years should in future pay no Turkish tax on foreign income and capital gains for 20 years. Every structural analysis runs through multiple paths — including when relocation becomes tax-expensive.

pricing.title: Choose Your Analysis Package
pricing.subtitle: Choose the package that best fits your situation — from single-topic analysis to full structural review.

services.title: Structured. Calculated. Documented.
services.description: The new Turkish tax regime for entrepreneurs, founders and family offices.

faq.title: What you really want to know.
faq.description: Precise answers — the current state of affairs, without promises the law doesn't yet provide.

items.question: What does the new Turkish tax regime mean specifically?
items.answer: Those who have not been tax-resident in Turkey for the past three years should in future pay no Turkish tax on foreign income and capital gains for 20 years — only income from Turkish sources would remain taxable. The package was announced in April 2026. As of June 2026: in parliamentary process. The final legislation and administrative practice will be decisive.

items.question: Does this apply to my GmbH shares or a company sale?
items.answer: Capital gains from foreign sources are the core of the regime. The timing is crucial: the Austrian exit tax on capital assets is linked to the relocation itself and must be assessed separately — that is exactly what we calculate in the structural analysis before anyone assumes exemption.

items.question: How are dividends from my US or foreign company treated?
items.answer: Foreign dividends generally fall under the favoured income. The company itself is not affected: a US Corporation continues to pay US corporate tax on its worldwide income. The regime operates at the level of the natural person — not at the company level.

items.question: Does the tax exemption apply to cryptocurrencies?
items.answer: The announced law speaks generally of foreign income and capital gains — without an explicit crypto exception. Whether and how a crypto portfolio falls under this is a question of classification and later administrative practice, and must be checked on a case-by-case basis. We do not promise what the law does not provide.

items.question: Do I have to live permanently in Turkey?
items.answer: Tax residency is generally linked to a stay of more than six months in the calendar year. How to credibly establish domicile and the centre of life interests is part of the analysis.

items.question: What remains taxable in Austria?
items.answer: Domestic sources remain taxable — such as rental income from Austrian real estate or income from domestic participations. Relocation does not automatically shift everything. Which income streams are affected is clarified in the structural analysis.

register.title: Clarity begins with an analysis.
register.description: Two minutes suitability check. Then book the package that fits your situation.
register.successText: We have received your registration and will be in touch shortly.

The content of this page serves for general information and does not replace a case-specific review.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.section1Text: Our website, https://dynamicresidency.com (hereinafter: "the website") uses cookies and other related technologies (for convenience all technologies are referred to as "cookies"). Cookies are also placed by third parties we have engaged. In the document below we inform you about the use of cookies on our website.
privacyPolicy.section2Text: A cookie is a small simple file that is sent along with pages of this website and stored by your browser on the hard drive of your computer or another device. The information stored therein may be returned to our servers or to the servers of the relevant third parties during a subsequent visit.
privacyPolicy.section3Text: A script is a piece of program code that is used to make our website function properly and interactively. This code is executed on our server or on your device.
privacyPolicy.section4Text: A web beacon (or a pixel tag) is a small, invisible piece of text or image on a website that is used to monitor traffic on a website. In order to do this, various data about you is stored using web beacons.
privacyPolicy.section5Text: 5.1 Technical or functional cookies. Some cookies ensure that certain parts of the website work properly and that your user preferences remain known. By placing functional cookies, we make it easier for you to visit our website. This way, you do not need to repeatedly enter the same information when visiting our website and, for example, the items remain in your shopping cart until you have paid. We may place these cookies without your consent.
privacyPolicy.section6Text: When you visit our website for the first time, we will show you a pop-up with an explanation about cookies. As soon as you click on "Save preferences", you consent to us using the categories of cookies and plug-ins you selected in the pop-up, as described in this Cookie Policy. You can disable the use of cookies via your browser, but please note that our website may no longer work properly. Functional: The technical storage or access is strictly necessary for the legitimate purpose of enabling the use of a specific service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user, or for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network.
privacyPolicy.section7Title: 7. Your rights with respect to personal data
privacyPolicy.section7Text: You have the following rights with respect to your personal data: You have the right to know why your personal data is needed, what will happen to it, and how long it will be retained for. Right of access: You have the right to access your personal data that is known to us. Right to rectification: you have the right to supplement, correct, have deleted or blocked your personal data whenever you wish. If you give us your consent to process your data, you have the right to revoke that consent and to have your personal data deleted. Right to transfer your data: you have the right to request all your personal data from the controller and transfer it in its entirety to another controller. Right to object: you may object to the processing of your data. We comply with this, unless there are justified grounds for processing. To exercise these rights, please contact us at the contact details at the bottom of this Policy.
privacyPolicy.section8Text: You can use your internet browser to automatically or manually delete cookies. You can also specify that certain cookies may not be placed. Another option is to change the settings of your internet browser so that you receive a message each time a cookie is placed. For more information about these options, please refer to the instructions in the Help section of your browser. Please note that our website may not work properly if all cookies are disabled. If you do delete the cookies in your browser, they will be placed again after your consent when you visit our websites again.
privacyPolicy.section9Text1: For questions and/or comments about our Cookie Policy and this statement, please contact us by using the following contact details:

closing.title: Check your structure before you act
closing.p1: Dynamic Residency helps entrepreneurs and investors from across Europe find out whether their plan actually works — before making decisions that can no longer be reversed later.
closing.p2: Sound decisions emerge from the interplay of European and Turkish tax law combined with individual advice.
closing.highlight: Turkey is not the challenge. The structure is.
